body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor` package following a PR that upgraded the API version to `2025-05-01-preview` and introduced five new resource types. The gaps include missing README code snippets, missing sample test files, and a sparse CHANGELOG entry.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor` (version `1.0.0-beta.6` unreleased)
**Affected API:** New resource types: `AdvisorAssessmentResource`, `AdvisorResiliencyReviewResource`, `AdvisorTriageResource`, `AdvisorTriageRecommendationResource`, `AdvisorScoreEntityResource`
**Scenarios:**
- README `Examples` section contains only a generic link with no inline code snippets for any resource type
- No sample test files exist for the five new resource types under `sdk/advisor/.../tests/Samples/`
- CHANGELOG entry for `1.0.0-beta.6 (Unreleased)` is sparse â€” only mentions API version upgrade without naming the new resource types

**Root ask:** Add itemized CHANGELOG entries for the new resource types, create sample test files demonstrating common usage patterns, and update the README with code snippet placeholders (then inject with `Update-Snippets.ps1`).
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue â€” no runtime bug to reproduce. The issue was generated by the "Update Docs" automation workflow referencing PR #58230.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify the five new resource types are present in the generated code under `sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/src/Generated/`
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md` under the `1.0.0-beta.6 (Unreleased)` heading with an itemized list of the new resource types
3. Create sample test files in `sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/tests/Samples/` following the pattern of existing samples (e.g., `Sample_SuppressionContractCollection.cs`)
4. Add snippet placeholders to `README.md` `## Examples` section and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 advisor` to inject code
5. Build and run non-live tests: `dotnet build sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/ && dotnet test sdk/advisor/Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` â€” The issue explicitly references `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor`, which is a management-plane SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.*` naming convention)
- **Service:** `Advisor` â€” The package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Advisor` in the `/sdk/advisor/` service directory
- **Confidence:** High â€” Package name is explicitly stated in the issue body with unambiguous service and category mapping; aligns with existing `%Advisor` and `%Mgmt` service labels in CODEOWNERS
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) â€” The issue has the "Mgmt" label; bottom-to-top scan encountered this catch-all before the `%Advisor`-only entry at line 533
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed (the `%Mgmt` catch-all entry has only AzureSdkOwners)
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978` (single AzureSdkOwner); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** `%Advisor %Mgmt` combined entry â€” does not exist in CODEOWNERS, so no specific override applied. Various `%X %Mgmt` entries (lines 915â€“1221) skipped because none list "Advisor" as a required label. `%Advisor` entry at line 533 (ServiceOwners: @mojayara @Prasanna-Padmanabhan) was never reached because `%Mgmt` catch-all at line 903 matched first during bottom-to-top scan.
</details>
